architecture.summary: Tool-MVR is an open-source LLM (Qwen-7B) finetuned on two high-quality instruction datasets to improve tool-augmented reasoning. The system operates in two stages: (1) Multi-Agent Meta-Verification (MAMV) validates APIs, queries, and reasoning trajectories offline to construct ToolBench-V, eliminating hallucinated API calls from training data; (2) Exploration-based Reflection Learning (EXPLORE) uses tool feedback in a dynamic 'Error → Reflection → Correction' loop to construct ToolBench-R, enabling the model to recover from tool invocation failures. Information flow: raw tool-use instructions → MAMV validation pipeline → cleaned ToolBench-V → finetuning + parallel EXPLORE feedback collection → ToolBench-R → combined finetuning → inference with improved tool planning and reflection.
trained_components: base LLM (Qwen-7B) finetuning on ToolBench-V and ToolBench-R
tool reflection capability via EXPLORE dataset

biblio.title: Advancing Tool-Augmented Large Language Models via Meta-Verification and Reflection Learning

claims.statement: Tool-MVR achieves state-of-the-art performance on StableToolBench, surpassing both ToolLLM (by 23.9%) and GPT-4 (by 15.3%)
On RefineToolBench, Tool-MVR achieves a 58.9% error correction rate
Current models face weak tool reflection abilities: over 90% of errors cannot be corrected resulting from static imitation learning

Multi-Agent Meta-Verification (MAMV) rigorously validates APIs, queries, and reasoning trajectories to construct ToolBench-V, a new high-quality instruction dataset that addresses the limitation of unreliable tool planning and invocation
Exploration-based Reflection Learning (EXPLORE) enhances tool reflection capabilities by leveraging tool feedback through a dynamic 'Error → Reflection → Correction' learning paradigm
Tool-MVR demonstrates strong generalization capabilities across unseen tools and scenarios
